reasoning: France enters the knockouts as the clear market favorite after a perfect group stage, with bookmakers pricing them around +300 to +350 and oddsmakers calling them the deepest, most talented squad in the field; their formula of absorbing pressure and exploding into transition through Mbappe, Dembele and Olise has produced a 10-2 scoreline across recent halves. Argentina is the main threat as defending champion, winning all three group games behind a sharp Messi and drawing the easier side of the bracket, which has lifted them to roughly +400-430 and co-favorite status to reach the final. Spain and England are the next tier with single-digit odds, while Brazil under Ancelotti and Germany carry talent but have shown vulnerability (Brazil's opening draw with Morocco, Germany's loss to Ecuador). Morocco is the live outsider: elite defensive organization that has long been their hallmark, plus a possible altitude/host-adjacent edge if they navigate the Netherlands in Monterrey.
